mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is designed for ease of deployment and management using Juju. It is ideal for users looking for a robust Kubernetes solution that integrates well with Ubuntu and offers modern metrics and monitoring capabilities. Key trade-offs include the requirement of Juju for deployment and management, which may add complexity for users unfamiliar with it.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is the tool used to deploy and manage Charmed Kubernetes. This step installs Juju on your system.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the Juju controller, which is necessary for managing the Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju bootstrap ubuntu-cloud --config image=ubuntu:22.04

```

> Replace 'ubuntu-cloud' with your desired cloud provider.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle, which includes all necessary components for a Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> This will deploy the latest version of Charmed Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
After deployment, this command checks the status of the Kubernetes components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Look for all units in the 'active' state.

steps.title: Configure Kubernetes Access
steps.description:
This step sets up your kubeconfig file to access the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju scp kubernetes-control-plane/leader:config ~/.kube/config

```

> This command retrieves the kubeconfig from the control plane leader unit.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

juju destroy-controller --destroy-all-models

```

> This will remove the Juju controller and all models.

uninstall.title: Cleanup Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure that any cloud resources created during the deployment are also cleaned up to avoid incurring costs.

```bash

juju remove-application charmed-kubernetes

```

> This command will remove the application from the Juju model.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configuration and data. This step will upgrade the Charmed Kubernetes deployment to the latest version.

```bash

juju upgrade-charm charmed-kubernetes

```

> This command upgrades the Charmed Kubernetes charm to the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the Kubernetes components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Look for all units in the 'active' state after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Backup Configuration
upgrade.description:
It's important to backup your configuration before performing an upgrade.

```bash

juju scp kubernetes-control-plane/leader:config kubeconfig-backup.yaml

```

> Backup your kubeconfig to a file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the specified cloud provider is not available or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud provider configuration and ensure it is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubernetes-control-plane not responding'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the Kubernetes master is not running or has failed.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju status' to check the status of the Kubernetes master and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju deploy failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are insufficient resources in the cloud environment.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your cloud environment has enough resources (CPU, RAM) to deploy Charmed Kubernetes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeconfig not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig file has not been generated or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju scp kubernetes-control-plane/leader:config ~/.kube/config' to generate the kubeconfig file.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features, including improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that all charms are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'juju status' indicating that all units are in the 'active' state, confirming that Charmed Kubernetes is running correctly.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Juju installed and a compatible cloud environment ready for deployment.
